pr.title: sobjlib: decompile SObj setup and render paths
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Merged SObjLib decompilation PR implementing the SObj setup path and most of the SObj render path. The PR body reports HSD_SObjLib_803A477C and HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC at 100%, HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68 as a 99.88707% near-match, and .data/.sdata2 at 100%. It also added SObj descriptor/color typing and corrected Dolphin GX texture format typing so CI texture setup can use GXTexFmt directly.

key_files.role: Main implementation: added SOBJLIB_INTERNAL, decompiled HSD_SObjLib_803A477C, near-matched HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68, added SObjLib constants, and changed HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC far_val to load the 2.0F constant through HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98.
Added HSD_SObjDesc, GXColor union overlays for SObj fields at 0x38 and 0x3C, and an internal typed prototype for HSD_SObjLib_803A477C gated by SOBJLIB_INTERNAL.
Folded CI texture format values GX_TF_C4, GX_TF_C8, and GX_TF_C14X2 into GXTexFmt and removed separate GXCITexFmt typedef.
Changed GXInitTexObjCI format parameter from GXCITexFmt to GXTexFmt.
Updated GXInitTexObjCI definition to match the GXTexFmt signature.
Removed the now-unnecessary GXCITexFmt cast when passing imagedesc->format to GXInitTexObjCI.
Added DisableFormat: true and SortIncludes: false for the extern tree.

what_changed: Implemented HSD_SObjLib_803A477C as a typed SObj setup/allocation routine: selects primary/secondary image descriptors, allocates via HSD_ObjAlloc, asserts on NULL, initializes GX texture/TLUT objects, initializes SObj fields/colors/flags, computes normalized texture coordinates from GX texture object dimensions, links the SObj to its GObj with HSD_SObjLib_803A44D4, and returns the new SObj.

Implemented HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68 as a large GX render routine and documented it as a 99.88707% near-match in the PR body. The code handles SObj flags at x40, optional Z texture/secondary texture handling, TLUT loading for CI formats, TEV stage setup, alpha/blend state, format-specific TEV color/alpha cases, rotated/scaled quad vertex emission, and render-state cleanup.

Completed HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC according to the PR body. The diff changes far_val from literal 2.0f to a volatile load from HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98, while the surrounding code builds an orthographic CObj/viewport/scissor setup and installs the GX link.
Added SObjLib constants HSD_SObjLib_804DEA80 through HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98, including packed color constants and f32 constants 0.5F and 2.0F, replacing the prior data_pad_jumptable placeholder.
Added HSD_SObjDesc with fields image, tlut, and image2, allowing the setup function to use HSD_ImageDesc and HSD_Tlut types instead of opaque integer arguments internally.
Changed SObj color storage at offsets 0x38 and 0x3C into unions that preserve raw byte fields x38-x3F while also allowing GXColor access as x38_color and x3C_color.
Used SOBJLIB_INTERNAL so sobjlib.c sees the accurate typed HSD_SObjLib_803A477C prototype while non-internal includes still see the older placeholder int-based signature.
Unified GX CI texture formats under GXTexFmt by removing GXCITexFmt and updating GXInitTexObjCI plus the TObj call site.
Added extern/.clang-format to disable formatting and include sorting under extern, keeping external SDK-like files from being reformatted by local tooling.
Verification in the PR body included pre-commit on sobjlib files, configure.py plus ninja via wibo, sobjlib.o build, and objdiff results for the touched functions/data.
smart_moves: The SOBJLIB_INTERNAL gate let the PR introduce accurate internal types for decompilation without forcing a broad public prototype churn at all call sites.
The GXColor union overlays improved readability for TEV color calls while preserving byte-accurate SObj layout and existing xNN field access.
Unifying GXCITexFmt into GXTexFmt removed casts at real call sites and matched the fact that HSD_ImageDesc->format is passed to both regular and CI texture initialization.
The volatile const f32 load from HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98 is a targeted matching fix for HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC, avoiding a literal 2.0f codegen shape that did not match.
The render implementation keeps explicit GX state setup and cleanup in one path, including HSD_StateInvalidate, HSD_StateInitTev, HSD_ClearVtxDesc, and restoring Z mode after drawing.
The PR reported exact and near-match percentages, plus a build caveat for the known Wine server crash path, making the matching status auditable.
decomp_lessons: For baselib render functions, matching requires reconstructing the full GX/TEV state machine and the cleanup tail, not just the draw calls.
When a struct field is byte-initialized but later consumed as a GXColor, a union overlay can be the least invasive way to improve type correctness without changing layout.
Header/API typing can be a decomp blocker: if a shared image format field feeds both GXInitTexObj and GXInitTexObjCI, keeping CI formats in GXTexFmt can reduce casts and produce cleaner code.
Internal-only typed prototypes are useful when a function is decompiled before all external call sites have enough type information.
Small constant-load choices matter for exact matching; referencing an address-named f32 through a volatile const pointer can force the desired load sequence.
Near-matches should preserve the exact objdiff percentage in the PR record; here HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68 is explicitly not claimed as a full match.
naming_conventions: The PR kept address-based function names such as HSD_SObjLib_803A477C, HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68, and HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC rather than inventing semantic names.
New descriptor typing uses HSD_SObjDesc with simple field names image, tlut, and image2.
Unknown SObj fields remain xNN-style, while typed overlays use suffixes such as x38_color and x3C_color.
Address-based data symbols HSD_SObjLib_804DEA80 through HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98 remain unnamed semantically, reflecting limited evidence about their meaning.
SOBJLIB_INTERNAL is used as the macro name for internal-only type exposure.
CI texture formats are represented as GX_TF_C4, GX_TF_C8, and GX_TF_C14X2 inside GXTexFmt; GXCITexFmt was removed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68 uses PAD_STACK(0x20), indicating an explicit stack-shape tactic for the near-match.
HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC uses far_val = *(volatile const f32*) &HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98 to force a load of the 2.0F constant from the named data symbol.
Packed constants are moved into GXColorS10/GXColor via u32 pointer casts before GXSetTevColorS10 and GXSetTevKColor, matching the data/register setup more directly than field-by-field assignment.
The render path uses explicit local ordering for sinf/cosf-derived half-angle products and emits GXPosition2f32/GXTexCoord2f32 calls in quad order, preserving the observed geometry code shape.
The TLUT load condition is expressed as (u8)(tex_fmt - GX_TF_C4) <= 1U, a compact range check over CI texture formats in the decompiled render routine.
Replacing the data_pad_jumptable placeholder with real constants and the decompiled switch/state logic contributed to .data/.sdata/.sdata2 matching reported by the PR/bot.
review_feedback: No human reviews or review comments were present in the slice.
decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code 66.75% (+0.02%, +612 bytes) and matched data 39.02% (+0.03%, +304 bytes).
The bot listed new matches for HSD_SObjLib_803A477C, .data, .sdata2, and .sdata, plus an improvement for HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68 from 0.00% to 99.89%.
follow_up_queries: Did a later PR exact-match HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68 beyond 99.88707%?
Were the SObj x40 flag bits 1, 2, 4, 8, and 0x10 later given semantic names?
Did later SObj work rename image2 or clarify the secondary texture/Z texture behavior?
Did the GXTexFmt versus GXCITexFmt consolidation require fixes in other Dolphin GX call sites?
Are there other exact-match fixes using volatile const f32 loads from address-named .sdata2 symbols?
